stories.title: Born in south Lebanon, displaced to Beirut, two grandmothers reflect on Israeli invasions
stories.description: They grew up amid olive groves in southern Lebanon. The son of one married the other's daughter. Now they're living temporarily in a vacant building in central Beirut, displaced many times.

stories.title: Iran says it has closed the Strait of Hormuz again, as ceasefire nears its end
stories.description: Iran's military said on Saturday the Strait of Hormuz has "returned to its previous state." The announcement came after President Trump had said the blockade on Iranian ports would remain in place.

stories.title: What's it like to negotiate with Iran? We asked people who have done it
stories.description: Experts who spent months negotiating a 2015 nuclear agreement with Iran say mutual mistrust, starkly different negotiating styles, and the complexity of the issues make a quick deal unlikely.

stories.title: Hormuz reopens, but insurers aren't ready to sound the all-clear
stories.description: Insurance for cargo and oil vessels stuck in the Strait of Hormuz has skyrocketed. How can insurers help us understand the realities of the war with Iran?

stories.title: Gasoline could drop below $4 in coming days
stories.description: Gasoline costs should start to fall soon, although a full recovery to pre-war prices is expected to take months. That's assuming that peace holds and traffic flows resume through the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Iran says Strait of Hormuz is 'open' but tracking shows few ships moving
stories.description: US President Donald Trump welcomed Tehran's announcement but said the US naval blockade would continue until a peace deal was reached.

stories.title: A moment of risk and opportunity: How two ceasefires could boost US-Iran talks
stories.description: Some kind of diplomatic progress is now under way, but is it enough to prevent a return to war?

stories.title: Ceasefire with Israel brings respite to Lebanon, but obstacles to peace remain
stories.description: The halt to weeks of fighting is being celebrated in Lebanon, but it will be difficult to ensure it lasts.

stories.title: What we know about the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
stories.description: A 10-day truce between the two countries is now in effect, with Iran-backed Hezbollah voicing support, as negotiations continue between the US and Iran.

stories.title: Lyse Doucet: Under fragile ceasefire, Iranians wonder if US deal can be done
stories.description: The BBC's chief international correspondent reports from Iran as diplomatic efforts to avoid a return to war intensify.

stories.title: Israeli triple-tap strike kills three paramedics in Lebanon, officials say
stories.description: Lebanon's government condemns as a "flagrant crime" the killing of the paramedics, one of whom featured in a BBC report.

stories.title: Israel and Hezbollah continue attacks after Israel-Lebanon talks in US
stories.description: Israel strikes hit southern Lebanon and Hezbollah fires rockets at Israel, a day after rare direct negotiations.

stories.title: Sri Lanka repatriates 238 Iranian sailors stranded after US torpedo attack
stories.description: The survivors of the US attack on Iranian vessel Iris Dena, which claimed the lives of 104 people, were among those sent home.

stories.title: Lebanon seeks peace, but Hezbollah needs to be convinced first
stories.description: The Lebanese government go into peace talks with limited influence over the group.

stories.title: The Israeli town on the frontline with Hezbollah
stories.description: BBC foreign correspondent Nick Beake visits Metula, an Israeli town surrounded on three sides by Lebanon.

stories.title: Hormuz tensions highlight pressure tactics in US-Iran negotiations
stories.description: H. A. Hellyer, senior fellow in geopolitics at the Royal United Services Institute, was France 24’s guest and analysed the renewed tensions between the United States and Iran in the Strait of Hormuz as a strategy of mutual pressure amid ongoing negotiations. While talks are set t...

stories.title: French soldier killed in attack on UN mission in southern Lebanon, officials say
stories.description: A French soldier was killed and three others wounded ​while clearing a road in southern Lebanon in an attack that UNIFIL peacekeepers and French officials said on Saturday was likely carried out ​by ‌Iran-backed Hezbollah. In calls with Lebanese President ⁠Joseph Aoun and Prime M...

stories.title: Strait of Hormuz : Iran to block transit until US lifts its blockade
stories.description: Tensions continue to rise in the Middle East, with Iran briefly reopening the Strait of Hormuz before closing it again after around 20 hours. Tehran says the US is not respecting the terms of the agreement reached, casting further doubt on the fragile deal. Meanwhile, negotiation...

stories.title: Middle East : Fragile US-Iran talks hinge on nuclear and Hormuz disputes
stories.description: US-Iran negotiations show limited progress but remain tense, with disputes over enriched uranium and the Strait of Hormuz still unresolved. Donald Trump warns the conflict could resume if no deal is reached, while mediators say compromises may still be possible on nuclear restric...

stories.title: In Liberia, concerns are growing over gold mining pollution
stories.description: In Liberia, communities are raising alarm over rising water and soil pollution linked to gold mining activities. In the village of Jikandor, north of the capital, residents accuse the Bea Mountain Mining Corporation of violating environmental standards and contaminating rivers th...

stories.title: The Gaza Strip grapples with a major garbage, health crisis
stories.description: In Deir al-Balah, displaced families are living next to growing rubbish piles as destroyed infrastructure forces authorities to rely on unsafe temporary dumps. Across the Gaza Strip, poor sanitation and pest infestations are raising serious health concerns despite limited humanit...

stories.title: Middle East : What to expect from the truce between Lebanon and Israel ?
stories.description: The United States and Israel announced on Thursday evening that a ceasefire agreement had been reached in Washington between Lebanon and Israel, following historic direct talks between the two countries. Dany Moudallal, a journalist at the Lebanese daily L’Orient-Le Jour, spoke t...

stories.title: Iran closes Hormuz Strait again over US blockade with ships mid-transit
stories.description: Iran's military declared the Strait of Hormuz closed again on Saturday, its military command said, hours after reopening it and with more than a dozen commercial ships passing through the vital waterway. The toing and froing over the strait cast doubt on US President Donald Trump...

stories.title: Trump claims on Iranian concessions trigger questions, rejections in Tehran
stories.description: The political drama culminates with the IRGC announcing that the Strait of Hormuz is once again restricted.

stories.title: Iran’s supreme leader warns of ‘new bitter defeats’ for US and Israel
stories.description: Iran’s Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei warned the navy was ready to inflict ‘new bitter defeats’ on the US and Israel.

stories.title: Iran’s deputy FM says no date for more US talks until ‘framework’ agreed
stories.description: Iran’s Deputy Foreign Minister said talks with the US cannot happen until the two sides agree on a shared ‘framework'.

stories.title: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz again over US blockade of its ports
stories.description: Reports of Iranian gunboats opening fire on tanker after Tehran says it is closing waterway until US lifts its blockade.

stories.title: Gunfire reported by vessel in Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: A maritime agency reported that a tanker was fired on by gunboats in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Turkiye woos investors amid Iran war fallout in Gulf economies
stories.description: Turkish officials are promoting Istanbul as a regional financial hub amid the war's fallout on the Gulf economies.

stories.title: US blockade of Iran needs to end before the Strait of Hormuz is fully reope
stories.description: Michael Shoebridge says US may be forced to end blockade of Iran to see the full reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Ambulance crew in south Lebanon describes Israel’s ‘triple-tap’ attack
stories.description: Ambulance workers in Lebanon recall Israeli ‘triple-tap’ targeted strikes which killed four paramedics and wounded six.

stories.title: Iran reimposes control over Strait of Hormuz as ships report gunfire
stories.description: A handful of oil and gas tankers crossed the Strait of Hormuz on Saturday during a brief reopening, tracking data showed, but others retreated and two were reportedly attacked as Iran closed the route again. The toing and froing over the strait cast doubt on US President Donald T...

stories.title: CDF Munir concludes 3-day Iran visit, discusses sustainable regional peace: ISPR
stories.description: Chief of Defence Forces (CDF) and Chief of the Army Staff (COAS) Field Marshal Asim Munir and Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi have concluded a three-day official visit to Iran, the Inter-Services Public Relations (ISPR) said on Saturday. The discussions focused on “bringing sustai...

stories.title: More than 38,000 girls, women killed in Gaza conflict, UN agency says
stories.description: GENEVA: Over 38,000 women and girls were killed in Gaza Strip by the end of last year, the UN estimated Friday, amounting to over half of the 71,000 deaths recorded by the territory’s health ministry since Oct 2023. “Between Oct 2023 and Dec 2025, more than 38,000 women and girls...

stories.title: Pakistan escorted Iran negotiators home amid Israel concerns
stories.description: • Sources say Islamabad deployed some two dozen jets for mission• Airborne Warning and Control System also used for aerial surveillance• Similar protection ‘may be offered to Iranian officials in next round of talks if needed’ ISLAMABAD: Pakistan Air Force escorted Iranian negoti...

stories.title: US cloud dominance risks European security: report
stories.description: PARIS: More than three-quarters of European countries use American cloud computing services for vital national security functions, a report published Friday found, warning that the dependency could make governments vulnerable. The analysis arrives as European governments increasi...

stories.title: Pakistan escorted Iranian negotiators home from peace talks amid concerns of Israeli attack, sources say
stories.description: The air force escorted Iranian negotiators home from inconclusive peace talks with the United States last weekend, launching a major operation after the Iranians said Israel might seek to kill them, three sources told Reuters. Pakistan deployed some two dozen jets in the escort, ...

stories.title: No date set for next round of Iran-U.S. talks: Iran Deputy Foreign Minister
stories.description: Saeed Khatibzadeh said both sides were currently focused on finalising a framework of understanding before proceeding with further negotiations

stories.title: Iran-Israel war LIVE: Two Indian vessels make reverse course in Strait of Hormuz following reports of gunfire
stories.description: Iran swiftly reversed course on reopening the Strait of Hormuz, reimposing restrictions on the critical waterway after the U.S. said the move would not end its blockade

stories.title: Iran's 'valiant navy' ready to inflict 'new bitter defeats' on its enemies: Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei
stories.description: Defiant message came as Iran swiftly reimposed restrictions on the Strait of Hormuz

stories.title: Two Indian ships reverse course in Hormuz strait, says vessel tracker
stories.description: Iran said earlier it was reimposing restrictions on the strait in response to a U.S. blockade on Iranian shipping and ports

stories.title: Iranian gunboats fire on tanker in Strait of Hormuz, British military says
stories.description: The United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations center said the tanker and crew were reported safe

stories.title: Iran reimposes restrictions on Strait of Hormuz, accusing U.S. of violating deal to reopen it
stories.description: Iran warned that it would continue to block transit through the strait as long as the U.S. blockade of Iranian ports remained in effect

stories.title: Iran partially reopens airspace for civilian traffic — air traffic controllers
stories.description: Namely, six civilian airports in Iran, including two in Tehran, will operate daily for 11 hours, the source specified

stories.title: French military killed in attack on peacekeepers in Lebanon — Macron
stories.description: Three more soldiers were wounded and evacuated, French President added

stories.title: Israeli army reports striking Lebanon as it accuses `terrorists’ of violating ceasefire
stories.description: The IDF did not specify their affiliation

stories.title: Senior Russian senator says he cannot imagine pro-American forces coming to power in Iran
stories.description: If this situation continues, it will be a political defeat for Washington, said the Chairman of the Federation Council's Information Policy Commission Alexey Pushkov
